message:
fix(channel): enforce &mut receiver for sequential recv operations

Change recv(&self) → recv(&mut self) and try_recv(&self) → try_recv(&mut self)
across MPSC, oneshot, and session channels. This statically prevents concurrent
polls on the same receiver, tightening the compile-time contract: receivers are
logically sequential, and Rust's borrow checker now enforces single-pollee
invariants.

Affected APIs:
  - mpsc::Receiver::recv, try_recv, recv_timeout
  - oneshot::Receiver::recv, try_recv, recv_timeout
  - session::Receiver (borrow patterns in tests)

All test harnesses updated to declare receivers with explicit `mut` bindings:
  - tests/channel_conformance.rs: receiver fixture mutability
  - tests/e2e_messaging.rs: 4 channel setup sites updated
  - tests/repro_mpsc_blocking.rs: receiver binding mutability

Also simplifies oneshot internals by removing redundant atomic fences where
the &mut guarantee makes them unnecessary (-81 lines net reduction in oneshot).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
